1.Zach DezenzoRHBHOU vs Joe Ryan (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.236 / xBA 0.193 / xSLG 0.325 (-79 pts)Park -16 pts14d 0.284vs RHP 0.263K 33.8%BB 5.2%HR 0.2%Hit 19.8%Edge Pitcher

Zach Dezenzo RHB vs Joe Ryan RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 133+480+245, pitcher rows 750+2798+2108

Verdict

Ryan's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach Dezenzo should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.236 xwOBA (-79 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -31 pts (raw 0.288 -> adj 0.257); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.257 -> final 0.236); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 72.6 mph
swing length 7.5 ft
attack angle 11.7°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-3.8+6.849+3.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sweeper-7.1+22.810+15.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-4.9+4.831-0.1on plane
Slider-6.0+17.528+11.5swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Split-Finger-7.3+19.06+11.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Knuckle Curve-9.0+11.7*0+2.7on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 64.8 mph
swing length 6.6 ft
attack angle 7.5°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-3.8+5.6109+1.9on plane
Sweeper-7.1+14.127+7.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Knuckle Curve-9.0+7.5*4-1.5on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

3.Cam SmithRHBHOU vs Joe Ryan (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.252 / xBA 0.206 / xSLG 0.363 (-63 pts)Park -23 pts14d 0.272vs RHP 0.274K 33.8%BB 5.3%HR 2.4%Hit 18.2%Edge Pitcher

Cam Smith RHB vs Joe Ryan RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 740+2025+0, pitcher rows 750+2798+2108

Verdict

Ryan's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Cam Smith should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.252 xwOBA (-63 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.294 -> adj 0.281); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.281 -> final 0.252); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Cam Smith is on a 14-day slump (-58 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 148+1341+1826, pitcher rows 750+2798+2108

Verdict

Ryan's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Jake Meyers should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.255 xwOBA (-60 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.304 -> adj 0.283); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Sinker17.80.3180.326GB 59% / LD 20% / FB 19% / PU 2%0.336+180.3470.2760.4355.2
Sweeper16.30.3140.266GB 43% / LD 19% / FB 30% / PU 8%0.299-150.2500.1990.32838.0

Unknown0.00.3220.278GB 39% / LD 22% / FB 26% / PU 12%0.306-150.2690.2050.35523.5

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 65.5 mph
swing length 6.8 ft
attack angle 7.1°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-3.8+2.4105-1.3on plane
Sweeper-7.1+12.037+5.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-4.9+1.391-3.6swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Slider-6.0+11.884+5.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Split-Finger-7.3+16.512+9.2swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Knuckle Curve-9.0+11.48+2.5on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

6.Tristan GrayLHBMIN vs Mike Burrows (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.256 / xBA 0.220 / xSLG 0.368 (-59 pts)Park -16 pts14d —vs RHP —K 23.0%BB 8.6%HR 1.1%Hit 20.1%Edge Pitcher

Tristan Gray LHB vs Mike Burrows RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 330+315+123, pitcher rows 836+1542+68

Verdict

Burrows' curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Tristan Gray should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.256 xwOBA (-59 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -27 pts (raw 0.314 -> adj 0.287); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -15 pts (bb_type-adj 0.287 -> final 0.256); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball36.00.3110.330GB 32% / LD 28% / FB 30% / PU 10%0.295-160.3100.2360.3969.4
Changeup34.90.3240.286GB 46% / LD 28% / FB 17% / PU 10%0.268-560.2390.1800.29655.9
Curveball16.50.3040.331GB 49% / LD 26% / FB 25% / PU 0%0.287-170.3030.2330.40540.4
Slider9.30.3100.332GB 29% / LD 41% / FB 24% / PU 5%0.327+170.3440.2700.45839.3
Sinker3.30.3070.324GB 39% / LD 30% / FB 23% / PU 9%0.295-120.3040.2440.3838.2

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Sale's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Connor Norby should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.258 xwOBA (-57 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.277 -> final 0.258); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. 8.Chandler SimpsonLHBTB vs Shane Baz (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.258 / xBA 0.227 / xSLG 0.381 (-57 pts)Park -32 pts14d 0.258vs RHP 0.277K 14.8%BB 6.9%HR 0.0%Hit 20.4%Edge Pitcher

Chandler Simpson LHB vs Shane Baz RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 649+1518+0, pitcher rows 882+2724+1207

Verdict

Baz's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Chandler Simpson should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.258 xwOBA (-57 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.306 -> adj 0.294); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Other0.00.2950.314GB 39% / LD 25% / FB 25% / PU 12%0.303+80.3020.2490.3868.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

9.Vinnie PasquantinoLHBKC vs Connelly Early (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.258 / xBA 0.206 / xSLG 0.382 (-57 pts)Park -23 pts14d 0.272vs LHP —K 34.9%BB 3.2%HR 3.2%Hit 19.7%Edge Pitcher

Vinnie Pasquantino LHB vs Connelly Early LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 789+2588+2240, pitcher rows 798+401+0

Verdict

Early's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Vinnie Pasquantino should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.258 xwOBA (-57 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -8 pts (raw 0.289 -> adj 0.281); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Connelly Early is in a 14-day hot stretch (-55 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

10.Thomas SaggeseRHBSTL vs Carmen Mlodzinski (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.259 / xBA 0.239 / xSLG 0.346 (-56 pts)Park -22 pts14d —vs RHP —K 23.9%BB 5.8%HR 0.3%Hit 21.9%Edge Pitcher

Thomas Saggese RHB vs Carmen Mlodzinski RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 283+1186+189, pitcher rows 756+1648+871

Verdict

Mlodzinski's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Thomas Saggese should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.259 xwOBA (-56 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -25 pts (raw 0.313 -> adj 0.289); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.289 -> final 0.259); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Carmen Mlodzinski is in a 14-day rough patch (+49 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

11.Carter JensenLHBKC vs Connelly Early (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.260 / xBA 0.199 / xSLG 0.376 (-55 pts)Park -19 pts14d 0.249vs LHP —K 31.8%BB 4.7%HR 0.0%Hit 20.7%Edge Pitcher

Carter Jensen LHB vs Connelly Early LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 676+293+0, pitcher rows 798+401+0

Verdict

Early's sinker is the projected matchup advantage, while Carter Jensen should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.260 xwOBA (-55 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -16 pts (raw 0.295 -> adj 0.279); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Carter Jensen is on a 14-day slump (-40 pts vs season). Connelly Early is in a 14-day hot stretch (-55 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

12.Seiya SuzukiRHBCHC vs Kyle Harrison (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.260 / xBA 0.225 / xSLG 0.377 (-55 pts)Park -24 pts14d 0.243vs LHP —K 24.4%BB 12.3%HR 3.7%Hit 14.0%Edge Pitcher

Seiya Suzuki RHB vs Kyle Harrison LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 637+2841+2520, pitcher rows 696+592+2078

Verdict

Harrison's slurve is the projected matchup advantage, while Seiya Suzuki should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.260 xwOBA (-55 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -34 pts (raw 0.338 -> adj 0.304); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.304 -> final 0.260); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Seiya Suzuki is on a 14-day slump (-87 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Starling Marte RHB vs Connelly Early LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 204+1256+1634, pitcher rows 798+401+0

Verdict

Early's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Starling Marte should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.261 xwOBA (-54 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.317 -> adj 0.300); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.300 -> final 0.261); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Connelly Early is in a 14-day hot stretch (-55 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 14.Maikel GarciaRHBKC vs Connelly Early (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.263 / xBA 0.226 / xSLG 0.389 (-52 pts)Park -31 pts14d 0.331vs LHP —K 17.4%BB 9.2%HR 1.2%Hit 18.1%Edge Pitcher

Maikel Garcia RHB vs Connelly Early LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 774+2521+2434, pitcher rows 798+401+0

Verdict

Early's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Maikel Garcia should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.263 xwOBA (-52 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.298); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Connelly Early is in a 14-day hot stretch (-55 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

15.Jeremy PeñaRHBHOU vs Joe Ryan (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.263 / xBA 0.215 / xSLG 0.378 (-52 pts)Park -24 pts14d —vs RHP —K 26.0%BB 3.5%HR 2.5%Hit 19.4%Edge Pitcher

Jeremy Peña RHB vs Joe Ryan RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 208+1788+2309, pitcher rows 750+2798+2108

Verdict

Ryan's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Jeremy Peña should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.263 xwOBA (-52 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -23 pts (raw 0.309 -> adj 0.286); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

swing length 7.4 ft
attack angle 8.8°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-3.8+5.1121+1.3on plane
Sweeper-7.1+12.572+5.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

16.Kazuma OkamotoRHBTOR vs Cam Schlittler (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.263 / xBA 0.234 / xSLG 0.353 (-52 pts)Park -3 pts14d 0.305vs RHP 0.305K 27.3%BB 7.0%HR 5.0%Hit 16.3%Edge Pitcher

Kazuma Okamoto RHB vs Cam Schlittler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 837+0+0, pitcher rows 898+1448+0

Verdict

Schlittler's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Kazuma Okamoto should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.263 xwOBA (-52 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -19 pts (raw 0.312 -> adj 0.293); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -27 pts (bb_type-adj 0.293 -> final 0.263); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kazuma Okamoto is on a 14-day slump (-42 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

17.Bryce HarperLHBPHI vs Andrew Abbott (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.266 / xBA 0.208 / xSLG 0.338 (-49 pts)Park +2 pts14d 0.414vs LHP —K 22.8%BB 6.6%HR 4.7%Hit 17.3%Slight Pitcher

Bryce Harper LHB vs Andrew Abbott LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 765+2264+2593, pitcher rows 795+2677+2334

Verdict

Abbott's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Bryce Harper should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.266 xwOBA (-49 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -50 pts (raw 0.318 -> adj 0.268); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Andrew Abbott is in a 14-day hot stretch (-67 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball52.90.3490.315GB 37% / LD 25% / FB 27% / PU 10%0.304-450.3040.2340.37913.0
Sweeper37.90.3180.277GB 30% / LD 16% / FB 33% / PU 20%0.255-620.2170.1730.27250.6

Slider0.10.3190.289GB 36% / LD 21% / FB 29% / PU 14%0.292-270.2650.2080.33230.4

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

18.Davis SchneiderRHBTOR vs Cam Schlittler (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.267 / xBA 0.236 / xSLG 0.348 (-48 pts)Park -1 pts14d —vs RHP —K 27.4%BB 8.0%HR 3.0%Hit 17.3%Slight Pitcher

Davis Schneider RHB vs Cam Schlittler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 376+1115+1975, pitcher rows 898+1448+0

Verdict

Schlittler's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Davis Schneider should look to drive the sweeper. Projection: 0.267 xwOBA (-48 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -6 pts (raw 0.304 -> adj 0.298); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -30 pts (bb_type-adj 0.298 -> final 0.267); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Early's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Michael Massey should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.267 xwOBA (-48 pts vs league, Pitcher). Connelly Early is in a 14-day hot stretch (-55 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Curveball3.40.3160.331GB 40% / LD 14% / FB 38% / PU 8%0.318+20.3340.2330.5120.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

20.Otto LopezRHBMIA vs Chris Sale (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.267 / xBA 0.216 / xSLG 0.374 (-48 pts)Park -5 pts14d 0.295vs LHP —K 25.0%BB 4.4%HR 0.8%Hit 23.0%Slight Pitcher

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 733+2137+1547, pitcher rows 846+2031+2818

Verdict

Sale's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Otto Lopez should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.267 xwOBA (-48 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.283 -> final 0.267); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

22.Michael BuschLHBCHC vs Kyle Harrison (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.271 / xBA 0.236 / xSLG 0.342 (-44 pts)Park -23 pts14d 0.431vs LHP —K 25.3%BB 5.4%HR 4.3%Hit 17.0%Slight Pitcher

Michael Busch LHB vs Kyle Harrison LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 820+2419+2369, pitcher rows 696+592+2078

Verdict

Harrison's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Michael Busch should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.271 xwOBA (-44 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.305 -> adj 0.288); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.288 -> final 0.271); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Michael Busch is on a 14-day heater (+90 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 715+2403+1424, pitcher rows 696+592+2078

Verdict

Harrison's slurve is the projected matchup advantage, while Pete Crow-Armstrong should look to drive the changeup. Projection: 0.271 xwOBA (-44 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.303 -> adj 0.291); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 25.Mitch GarverRHBSEA vs Sean Burke (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.275 / xBA 0.232 / xSLG 0.405 (-40 pts)Park -9 pts14d —vs RHP —K 27.7%BB 7.9%HR 3.5%Hit 17.3%Slight Pitcher

Mitch Garver RHB vs Sean Burke RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 322+1205+1815, pitcher rows 790+2380+309

Verdict

Burke's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Mitch Garver should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.275 xwOBA (-40 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -26 pts (bb_type-adj 0.310 -> final 0.275); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Sean Burke is in a 14-day rough patch (+94 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Wacha's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Mickey Gasper should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.275 xwOBA (-40 pts vs league, Pitcher). window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 878+2839+2728, pitcher rows 696+592+2078

Verdict

Harrison's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Ian Happ should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.275 xwOBA (-40 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.305 -> final 0.275); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 71.1 mph
swing length 7.0 ft

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.1+2.698-1.5on plane
Slurve-7.6+7.0*0-0.6on plane
Changeup-6.3+12.868+6.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

29.Leo JiménezRHBMIA vs Chris Sale (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.276 / xBA 0.217 / xSLG 0.357 (-39 pts)Park -9 pts14d 0.364vs LHP —K 32.5%BB 6.0%HR 1.3%Hit 18.0%Slight Pitcher

Leo Jiménez RHB vs Chris Sale LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 206+144+847, pitcher rows 846+2031+2818

Verdict

Sale's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Leo Jiménez should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.276 xwOBA (-39 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +5 pts (raw 0.279 -> adj 0.285); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

30.Myles StrawRHBTOR vs Cam Schlittler (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.276 / xBA 0.237 / xSLG 0.338 (-39 pts)Park -1 pts14d —vs RHP —K 19.3%BB 5.2%HR 1.8%Hit 22.3%Slight Pitcher

Myles Straw RHB vs Cam Schlittler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 321+1178+14, pitcher rows 898+1448+0

Verdict

Schlittler's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Myles Straw should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.276 xwOBA (-39 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.299 -> adj 0.284); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.284 -> final 0.276); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

32.Jackson ChourioRHBMIL vs Edward Cabrera (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.277 / xBA 0.241 / xSLG 0.389 (-38 pts)Park -32 pts14d 0.240vs RHP 0.235K 25.8%BB 3.9%HR 4.2%Hit 19.8%Slight Pitcher

Jackson Chourio RHB vs Edward Cabrera RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 247+2228+2163, pitcher rows 813+2253+1734

Verdict

Cabrera's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jackson Chourio should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.277 xwOBA (-38 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -6 pts (raw 0.306 -> adj 0.300); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.300 -> final 0.277); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jackson Chourio is on a 14-day slump (-68 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

33.Christian WalkerRHBHOU vs Joe Ryan (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.277 / xBA 0.207 / xSLG 0.381 (-38 pts)Park -16 pts14d 0.264vs RHP 0.276K 31.8%BB 4.5%HR 4.9%Hit 18.2%Slight Pitcher

Christian Walker RHB vs Joe Ryan RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 746+2519+2326, pitcher rows 750+2798+2108

Verdict

Ryan's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Christian Walker should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.277 xwOBA (-38 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -20 pts (raw 0.307 -> adj 0.287); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.287 -> final 0.277); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Christian Walker is on a 14-day slump (-72 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

34.Nico HoernerRHBCHC vs Kyle Harrison (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.277 / xBA 0.252 / xSLG 0.409 (-38 pts)Park -37 pts14d 0.302vs LHP —K 15.7%BB 5.9%HR 1.7%Hit 21.6%Slight Pitcher

Nico Hoerner RHB vs Kyle Harrison LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 785+2427+2319, pitcher rows 696+592+2078

Verdict

Projection: 0.277 xwOBA (-38 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.333 -> adj 0.315); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 67.5 mph
swing length 7.3 ft
attack angle 5.9°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.0+0.787-3.3swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Slurve-7.6+5.9*0-1.7on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

35.Samuel BasalloLHBBAL vs Steven Matz (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.277 / xBA 0.243 / xSLG 0.349 (-38 pts)Park -15 pts14d 0.350vs LHP —K 16.2%BB 3.4%HR 0.6%Hit 26.2%Slight Pitcher

Samuel Basallo LHB vs Steven Matz LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 509+443+0, pitcher rows 601+1197+758

Verdict

Matz's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Samuel Basallo should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.277 xwOBA (-38 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.307 -> adj 0.292); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Joey Ortiz RHB vs Edward Cabrera RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 432+1969+2178, pitcher rows 813+2253+1734

Verdict

Cabrera's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Joey Ortiz should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.278 xwOBA (-37 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +13 pts (raw 0.293 -> adj 0.307); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

37.Spencer TorkelsonRHBDET vs Tanner Bibee (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.278 / xBA 0.218 / xSLG 0.347 (-37 pts)Park -7 pts14d 0.215vs RHP 0.238K 26.0%BB 6.9%HR 3.5%Hit 18.1%Slight Pitcher

Spencer Torkelson RHB vs Tanner Bibee RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 798+2815+1716, pitcher rows 809+3062+3109

Verdict

Bibee's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Spencer Torkelson should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.278 xwOBA (-37 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -34 pts (raw 0.313 -> adj 0.279); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.279 -> final 0.278); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Spencer Torkelson is on a 14-day slump (-105 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

38.José AzócarRHBATL vs Janson Junk (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.279 / xBA 0.238 / xSLG 0.387 (-36 pts)Park -17 pts14d —vs RHP —K 18.7%BB 2.5%HR 0.0%Hit 27.4%Slight Pitcher

José Azócar RHB vs Janson Junk RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 62+67+287, pitcher rows 777+1622+175

Verdict

Junk's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while José Azócar should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.279 xwOBA (-36 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -8 pts (raw 0.304 -> adj 0.296); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Janson Junk is in a 14-day rough patch (+54 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 65.1 mph
swing length 6.6 ft
attack angle 1.9°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.6-0.511-5.1swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Slider-7.4+4.45-3.1swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Sweeper-7.6+1.9*4-5.7swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Changeup-7.4+1.9*0-5.5swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Curveball-9.8+1.9*0-7.9swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Sinker-4.9+1.9*5-3.0on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Matz's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Tyler O'Neill should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.279 xwOBA (-36 pts vs league, Pitcher). Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

40.Xavier EdwardsRHBMIA vs Chris Sale (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.279 / xBA 0.233 / xSLG 0.380 (-36 pts)Park -12 pts14d 0.332vs LHP —K 25.8%BB 6.2%HR 4.4%Hit 20.3%Slight Pitcher

Xavier Edwards RHB vs Chris Sale LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 798+2260+1192, pitcher rows 846+2031+2818

Verdict

Sale's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Xavier Edwards should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.279 xwOBA (-36 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +16 pts (raw 0.284 -> adj 0.301); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.301 -> final 0.279); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball43.80.3200.319GB 29% / LD 30% / FB 35% / PU 6%0.335+150.3400.2520.43810.1
Slider40.70.3180.239GB 40% / LD 21% / FB 30% / PU 8%0.328+100.2520.2000.29711.7

Sinker2.70.3110.330GB 40% / LD 25% / FB 30% / PU 6%0.337+260.3520.2690.4591.7

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 840+0+0, pitcher rows 809+3062+3109

Verdict

Bibee's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Kevin McGonigle should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.280 xwOBA (-35 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -25 pts (raw 0.324 -> adj 0.299); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Kevin McGonigle is on a 14-day slump (-57 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

43.James OutmanLHBMIN vs Mike Burrows (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.281 / xBA 0.255 / xSLG 0.393 (-34 pts)Park -12 pts14d —vs RHP —K 27.1%BB 8.7%HR 3.3%Hit 18.0%Slight Pitcher

James Outman LHB vs Mike Burrows RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 217+605+601, pitcher rows 836+1542+68

Verdict

Burrows' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while James Outman should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.281 xwOBA (-34 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +16 pts (raw 0.298 -> adj 0.314); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.314 -> final 0.281); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

44.Wenceel PérezRHBDET vs Tanner Bibee (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.282 / xBA 0.237 / xSLG 0.378 (-33 pts)Park -14 pts14d 0.190vs RHP 0.183K 20.6%BB 5.9%HR 2.8%Hit 21.3%Slight Pitcher

Wenceel Pérez RHB vs Tanner Bibee RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 388+1620+1829, pitcher rows 809+3062+3109

Verdict

Bibee's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Wenceel Pérez should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -6 pts (raw 0.302 -> adj 0.296); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Wenceel Pérez is on a 14-day slump (-77 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Cutter30.60.3130.290GB 34% / LD 20% / FB 28% / PU 18%0.287-260.2630.2020.35422.2
4-Seam Fastball26.90.3120.335GB 43% / LD 28% / FB 23% / PU 6%0.335+230.3540.3000.46811.1
Sinker18.90.3120.301GB 69% / LD 16% / FB 12% / PU 3%0.304-90.2890.2480.3213.5
Sweeper12.50.3070.293GB 37% / LD 12% / FB 39% / PU 12%0.287-200.2650.1910.34927.7

Curveball4.50.2970.302GB 41% / LD 18% / FB 27% / PU 13%0.284-130.2710.2090.33428.1

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

45.Nathan ChurchLHBSTL vs Carmen Mlodzinski (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.282 / xBA 0.257 / xSLG 0.406 (-33 pts)Park -22 pts14d 0.258vs RHP 0.276K 20.7%BB 5.2%HR 2.0%Hit 20.8%Slight Pitcher

Nathan Church LHB vs Carmen Mlodzinski RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 547+257+0, pitcher rows 756+1648+871

Verdict

Mlodzinski's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Nathan Church should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +8 pts (raw 0.312 -> adj 0.320); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.320 -> final 0.282); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Carmen Mlodzinski is in a 14-day rough patch (+49 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 46.Esteury RuizRHBMIA vs Chris Sale (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.282 / xBA 0.221 / xSLG 0.375 (-33 pts)Park -10 pts14d —vs LHP —K 31.2%BB 5.9%HR 4.5%Hit 17.1%Slight Pitcher

Esteury Ruiz RHB vs Chris Sale LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 123+88+246, pitcher rows 846+2031+2818

Verdict

Sale's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Esteury Ruiz should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +10 pts (raw 0.282 -> adj 0.292); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

47.Geraldo PerdomoLHBAZ vs Tyler Mahle (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.282 / xBA 0.208 / xSLG 0.368 (-33 pts)Park -2 pts14d 0.336vs RHP 0.302K 18.3%BB 9.7%HR 1.6%Hit 19.5%Slight Pitcher

Geraldo Perdomo LHB vs Tyler Mahle RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 754+3009+1580, pitcher rows 806+1381+229

Verdict

Mahle's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Geraldo Perdomo should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.300 -> adj 0.287); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Tyler Mahle is in a 14-day hot stretch (-62 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

48.Wilyer AbreuLHBBOS vs Michael Wacha (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.282 / xBA 0.240 / xSLG 0.378 (-33 pts)Park -21 pts14d 0.283vs RHP 0.326K 20.9%BB 6.9%HR 4.2%Hit 19.3%Slight Pitcher

Wilyer Abreu LHB vs Michael Wacha RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 767+1641+1806, pitcher rows 816+2666+2739

Verdict

Wacha's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Wilyer Abreu should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.311 -> adj 0.299); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Wilyer Abreu is on a 14-day slump (-69 pts vs season). Michael Wacha is in a 14-day hot stretch (-42 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

4-Seam Fastball47.50.3270.296GB 45% / LD 19% / FB 34% / PU 2%0.326-10.3080.2420.3598.5
Split-Finger39.40.3100.270GB 36% / LD 20% / FB 35% / PU 9%0.295-150.2490.1800.33152.9

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Sandy León RHB vs Janson Junk RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 41+41+0, pitcher rows 777+1622+175

Verdict

Junk's changeup is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.283 xwOBA (-32 pts vs league, Pitcher). Janson Junk is in a 14-day rough patch (+54 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.
